title: Path Core Snapshot

inspiration.source: Gamification with Purpose: What Learners Prefer to Motivate Their Learning
inspiration.note: Progress bars, concept maps, immediate feedback, and achievements were identified as preferred meaningful game design elements.

inspiration.source: Planning Gamification Strategies based on User Characteristics and DM
inspiration.note: Objectives, levels, progress, and choice appeared as important game element recommendations.

problem: The direct Path cockpit was visually denser after recent compaction, but the Core deck still mounted full Scan, Command, or Route panels inside a small bounded area. This created a cramped internal scroll feeling and weakened the first-screen game-board impression.
implementation: Added pathCoreSnapshotItems to derive three compact route, proof, and work/gate signal cards from lane data.
Added renderPathCoreSnapshot as a compact active-module lens with a route arc, signal cards, and fast actions.
Replaced the default Core panel body with the snapshot instead of mounting the full active module content.
Removed internal Core-panel scrolling in the Stage board.
Added mobile-specific compaction so the Core tabs and live rail fit inside the bounded board while the floating depth pill remains visible.
Fixed Mission Glance title clamping so long next actions stop stretching the mobile stage.

verification.result: failed before implementation because renderPathCoreSnapshot did not exist

nextAction: Continue upgrading the Path cockpit with higher-quality lane-specific motion and richer agent/level visuals while keeping the first screen compact.
